hook: Warmth and self-possession beat intensity in most early interactions.
body: When people feel both safe and respected, they are more willing to keep engaging and reveal personality.

hook: Trying to create attraction through sheer force of attention can read as pressure instead of magnetism.
body: Lead with ease, not hunger; confidence usually looks more spacious than urgent.

body: A relaxed smile and measured eye contact often outperform hard staring and over-serious focus.
